request.question: A-ha was founded by the keyboardest who was of what nationality?

title: Order of Fidelity
Magne Furuholmen
George Willcox
Federation of Gay Games
A-ha
International Boy Scouts, Troop 1
Liberian nationality law
List of A-ha tours
Mintohren
ECUARUNARI
sentences: The Order of Fidelity (Urdhëri Besa), also known as Order of Besa, was founded by King Zog I when he was President of Albania.
Founded on 22 December 1926, it was initially awarded in four classes (1.
Grand Cordon with star-Kordon i Madh me Yll 2.
Grand Officer-Oficer i Madh, 3.
Commander-Komandar, and 4.
Knight-Kalorës) and a medal, and was limited to six ordinary recipients of Albanian nationality, and not awardable to foreigners.
It was remodelled in 1932 and reduced to three classes (1.
Grand Cordon with star-Kordon i Madh me Yll 2.
Commander-Komandar, and 3.
Knight-Kalorës) and remained to be limited to only six ordinary recipients of Albanian nationality, and not made awardable to foreigners,
Magne Furuholmen (born 1 November 1962) is a Norwegian musician and visual artist.

Furuholmen, better known to music fans by his stage name Mags, is the keyboardist of the synthpop/rock band A-ha, and co-wrote many of the band's hits including "Take on Me", "Stay on These Roads", "Manhattan Skyline", "Cry Wolf", "Forever Not Yours", "Analogue (All I Want)", and "Foot of the Mountain".
George Willcox (born 1949) is an archaeobotanist who specialises in the origins of agriculture in the Near East.
He has been employed as researcher with the CNRS (Centre national de la recherche scientifique)France since 1989.
He has dual French and British nationality.

He studied environmental archaeology under the late Prof. Dimbleby at the Institute of Archaeology in London during the early 1970s and was then was employed as environmental archaeologist by the Museum of London.
In 1977 he married and left his job to follow his interest in Near Eastern archaeobotany.
An interest which he had acquired as an undergraduate working with Gordon Hillman in Turkey.

During the next few years he worked in Turkey, Afghanistan Jordan, Oman, Kuwait, Tajikistan, India and Syria and started amassing a charred archaeobotanical collection and modern reference collection.
These are now housed at the Muséum national d’Histoire naturelle in Paris.
It was during this somewhat precarious period that he moved to France and founded a family.

In 1984 Gordon Hillman introduced George to Jacques Cauvin and Patrica Anderson at the CNRS Institut de Préhistore Orientale in south east France where they developed a project on experimentally cultivating wild cereals In 1989 George entered the CNRS working on a number of early Neolithic sites in Syria in particular with Danielle Stordeur at Jerf el Amhar.
He lectured at several French universities on archaeobotany and the origins of agriculture and supervised post graduate students.
He was an associate editor for Vegetation History and Archaeobotany for many years and received an honorary doctorate from the University of Basel in 2013.
His 117 scholarly publications are a measure of his research achievements.

The Federation of Gay Games (FGG) is the sanctioning body of the Gay Games which was founded in 1982 by Tom Waddell, after he dealt first-hand with the prejudice towards gay athletes and their inability to openly participate in sporting events.
The Federation of Gay Games hosts the world's biggest cultural and sporting event for the LGBT community every four years.

The Federation of Gay Games is founded on the principles of "participation, inclusion, and personal best" and continues to support the LGBT community not only through the Gay Games but through scholarships for underprivileged members.
The Federation of Gay Games continues to expand throughout the world and accepts members of any sexual orientation and nationality to participate in the games or contribute.
The FGG is proudly hosting the next Gay Games in 2018 in Paris, France.
A-ha (usually stylized as a"-h"a; ] ) is a Norwegian band formed in Oslo in 1982.
The band was founded by Morten Harket (vocals), Magne Furuholmen (keyboards) and Pål Waaktaar-Savoy (guitars).
The group rose to fame during the mid-1980s after being discovered by musician and producer John Ratcliff, and continued global success in the 1990s and 2000s.

International Boy Scouts, Troop 1 was founded in 1911 as Japan's first Scout troop and, despite its early multinational character, was originally registered with the London headquarters of the Boy Scouts as "British Scouts in Foreign Countries", which was the only option at the time due to the Boy Scouts' "nationality requirement" and no international Boy Scout office.
The troop's character, and Scoutmaster, changed and it was recognized as a mixed-nationality, or "international" troop in 1918.
After formation of the World Scout Bureau, Troop 1 became the first "mixed nationality" group to be registered directly be the Bureau.
The troop has been continuously active since its foundation.

The troop's "mixed nationality" charter dated October 30, 1925 was issued after the newly formed International Bureau received approval to directly register such "international" groups at the 3rd World Scout Conference.
The charter was signed by Lord Baden-Powell.
The Republic of Liberia was founded by African slaves from North America by the American Colonization Society and returned to establish a republic on African soil.
Nationality law is given in the Aliens and Nationality Law of 1973, based on its 1847 Constitution.
Human rights activists have described current citizenship laws as explicitly racist, as it requires being black as a prerequisite to citizenship.
The first constitution allowed for women to transmit their nationality to their children, although multiple citizenship was not permitted nor is it permitted in revisions of the constitution.
A-ha is a Norwegian synthpop act, founded in 1982 by Morten Harket, Magne Furuholmen and Paul Waaktaar-Savoy in Oslo.
Since 1985, A-ha has performed throughout the world, including tours in North America, South America, Europe, Australia, Japan, and China.

Mintohren, also Mintōren, is a grass-roots movement created by young Zainichi Koreans to combat ethnic discrimination by encouraging a multicultural and positive approach to integration of Koreans into Japanese society.

The movement started in the 1970s as a joint effort of three groups: Mukuge no Kai, founded by Lee Kyung-jae in 1972 in Osaka, Tokkabi Kodomo Kai, founded in 1974 in Osaka, and Seikyu Sha, founded in 1974 in Tokyo.

In 1985, they began a campaign in Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture which resulted in the establishment of a division in the city board of education devoted exclusively to education for ethnic Koreans, and the elimination of the requirement that city employees hold Japanese nationality.

They also played an important role in the 1980s struggle against the requirement that all foreign residents, including Special Permanent Residents like the Zainichi Koreans, give their fingerprints to police; they organised refusers from different parts of Japan, as well as providing information and increasing awareness of their campaign.

ECUARUNARI (in Kichwa: "Ecuador Runakunapak Rikcharimuy ", "Movement of the indigenous people of Ecuador"), also known as Confederation of Peoples of Kichwa Nationality ("Ecuador Kichwa Llaktakunapak Jatun Tantanakuy ," in Spanish "Confederación de Pueblos de la Nacionalidad Kichwa del Ecuador ") is the organization of indigenous peoples of Kichwa nationality in the Ecuadorian central mountain region, founded in 1972.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Additional constraints (must follow):
- The first sentence must be an answer-first concise identification (a direct answer or concise identification). If the `context` already contains such a sentence, preserve it exactly unless a passage explicitly and directly contradicts it; otherwise derive that first sentence from the clearest passage.
- After the first sentence, append at most one short supporting sentence for each additional relevant named entity only when the passages supply corroborating attributes. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the inputs (context or passages) and paraphrase no more than one or two simple attributes.
- When a queried binary/status attribute for an entity is not stated in the provided passages, indicate that absence concisely within that entity's supporting sentence (for example, with a short parenthetical or clause saying the status is not given in the provided evidence).
- If the passages explicitly assert a relationship between two or more entities that is directly relevant to the question, include one short relationship sentence after the per-entity supporting sentence(s) describing that relationship; do not embed the same relationship claim redundantly in multiple sentences.
- If relevant entities exceed the available supporting-sentence budget under the 1–3 sentence limit, prioritize entities in this order: entities explicitly named in the question, entities named in the preserved first sentence, then other context entities. Do not exceed the 1–3 sentence cap.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Do not infer that the final state is unresolved merely because `summary_1` uses earlier unresolved or bridge language; prefer `summary_2` when it clarifies `summary_1`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

Binary detection is mandatory and must run first. Only if the question is not a binary yes/no or the binary detector does not apply, proceed to the extractive rules below. Use the extractor that matches the question intent and only apply an extractor when the summaries present the required adjacent or verbatim phrasing.

Extraction and formatting rules (apply only when binary detection does not produce a single-token answer):
- Person-name selection: if a longer verbatim personal-name string appears in either summary and contains a shorter candidate as a substring, emit the longest verbatim name exactly as it appears in the summaries. Prefer wording from `summary_2` when both summaries provide the same evidence but differ in form.
- Denomination/type extraction: for questions asking a "kind", "type", or "denomination" of a role, extract and emit exactly the minimal token or short phrase that appears immediately adjacent to the role word in the summaries (the word/phrase answering "what kind").
- Event-name extraction: when summaries show a contiguous proper-name followed by a generic event-type noun, emit only the contiguous proper-name core. Strip any leading articles and remove the trailing generic event-type noun; emit only the proper-name portion exactly as given in the summaries.
- Nationality handling: if both summaries unambiguously state the same nationality phrase and at least one summary places the literal single word "citizen" immediately after that nationality phrase, append exactly one space and the single word "citizen" to the nationality phrase. Otherwise emit the nationality phrase exactly as given in the summaries.

General formatting constraints for all outputs:
- Always emit the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries and do not add explanation, context, punctuation, or extra words.
- Do not introduce external facts, additional descriptors, dates, roles, or explanatory text not present in the summaries.
- Do not normalize or invent name variants that are not verbatim in the summaries.
- If the summaries conflict, lack the required adjacent phrasing, or do not present a clear verbatim form for the requested attribute, do not guess—use the module's normal unresolved output.

Output contract:
- Produce a single field `answer` whose contents follow the rules above. The `answer` field must contain only the minimal string supported by `summary_1` and `summary_2` and no additional tokens, punctuation, or explanation.

response.hop1_query: A-ha was founded by the keyboardest who was of what nationality?
hop1_docs: Chilean National Zoo | The Chilean National Zoo ("Zoológico Nacional de Chile") is a 4.8 ha zoo that was founded in 1925 in Santiago, Chile.  Located at the foot of San Cristóbal Hill in what is known as the Santiago Metropolitan Park ("Parque Metropolitano de Santiago"), the zoo is home to thousands of animals representing 158 species.  Unique exhibits feature Chilean native animals and birds including rare and endangered species.

Big Gus, What's the Fuss? | Big Gus, What's the Fuss?  (also known as Ha-Balash Ha'Amitz Shvartz and Fat Spy) was a 1973 Israeli comedy film directed by Ami Artzi and American director Lloyd Kaufman, the president of Troma Entertainment.  The plot revolves around a Hebrew detective named Gus and his partner Harry who are hired by a woman to investigate a possible case of adultery.

Hamra National Park | Hamra National Park (Swedish: "Hamra nationalpark" ) is a national park in Ljusdal Municipality, Gävleborg County, Sweden.  The park, which is a part of Orsa Finnmark (part of Dalarna), was founded in 1909 and was then 28 ha in area, but was extended in 2011 to 1383 ha .

Ha Hee-ra | Ha Hee-ra (born October 30, 1969) is a South Korean actress.  She was born in Seoul to a Taiwanese immigrant father and a Korean mother.  Ha began her acting career in 1981 when she was in the sixth grade, appearing mostly in films for teenagers.  In the 1990s, Ha became one of the top actresses in Korean television, along with Chae Shi-ra and Kim Hee-ae.  Among her notable dramas include "What Women Want" (1990), "What Is Love" (1991), "The Break of Dawn" (1993), "Catching Up with Gangnam Moms" (2007) and "President" (2010).

Murrumbidgee Valley National Park | The Murrumbidgee Valley National Park is a protected national park that is located in the Riverina region of New South Wales, in eastern Australia.  The 47703 ha national park is located approximately 6 km east of Hay , and approximately 15 km west of Narrandera .  The park protects part of what is now the largest continuous tract of river red gum forest in the world.

Dark Blue Dream | Dark Blue Dream is the second studio album by composer and keyboardest Steve Weingart.  Produced by Weingart, the album features guitarist Frank Gambale, bassist Jimmy Earl and drummer Tom Brechtlein.  The album was released worldwide in 2006 by Skeewa Music.

Isomgosiono | Isomgosiono, "Queen Anne", 1703 Treaty Signer for Pan NA Federation along with Sachem George Skipper of the Nottoway/Chero-en-ha-ka/Iroquois.  These pan Native American communities go back to what may be communities formed per the one reference on the Capt John Smith Map of 1656 listing a single Shawnee group in the Susquahanna Valley when he mapped the area at that time.  This map is found in the appendix of the Capt. John Smith Trail of the National Colonial Park System.  The parent of Queen Anne is of the Shawnee of that mapped valley and they had been brought over by Pardo in 1570, according to carbon dating of their settlement area (Langston-MOretti, National Park Service, Ch.  4, Archaeology Study ) along with the Old Cheraw, after Pardo conquered the 20,000 Wichita, Kansas Iowans who lived there.  Of those who were left, some were enslaved as "Monocan" (means Hemp Grower in Chiwere) and brought to the Susquahanna. (NPS Langston-Moretti, Ch.  4 and 10 of Weromicococo State Park/National Colonial Park Archaeology Study).  The Cheraw were listed as Xualla by Pardo, Sara by the Tazewell, TN group found by Pardo, and Charra by cartographers of the blended 1724 encampment of surviving small pox plaqued tribes who had to put aside their differences who would not normally have lived near each other.
response.summary_1:
Header: Keyboardist mention in passages (Steve Weingart); A-ha / A-ha keyboardist not present.

- Facts supported verbatim by passages:
  - "Dark Blue Dream is the second studio album by composer and keyboardest Steve Weingart."
  - "the album... was released worldwide in 2006 by Skeewa Music." 

- Ambiguities / missing facts:
  - Missing fact: the passages do NOT mention A-ha, A-ha's keyboardist, or that person's nationality (bridge entity: "A-ha keyboardist" — name unspecified in passages).
  - Candidate "keyboardest" entities: (1) Steve Weingart (present in passages) — ambiguous whether this is the intended keyboardist for A-ha; (2) the actual A-ha keyboardist (likely Magne Furuholmen) — NOT present in passages.

- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives:
  1. Fetch "A-ha" (band) page — inspect lede/infobox and "History"/"Members" or "Band members" sections for founding members and keyboardist.
  2. Fetch "Magne Furuholmen" page — inspect lede/infobox and "Early life"/"Career" for nationality.
  3. If needed, search keywords: "A-ha founding members keyboardist nationality" and "Magne Furuholmen nationality".
hop2_docs: Magne Furuholmen | Magne Furuholmen (born 1 November 1962) is a Norwegian musician and visual artist.  Furuholmen, better known to music fans by his stage name Mags, is the keyboardist of the synthpop/rock band A-ha, and co-wrote many of the band's hits including "Take on Me", "Stay on These Roads", "Manhattan Skyline", "Cry Wolf", "Forever Not Yours", "Analogue (All I Want)", and "Foot of the Mountain".

Past Perfect Future Tense | Past Perfect Future Tense is the first solo album of Norwegian band a-ha's Magne Furuholmen as Magne F. Magne worked in this album with Coldplay members, Will Champion and Guy Berryman, also Travis member, Andy Dunlop.
A Dot of Black in the Blue of Your Bliss | A Dot Of Black In The Blue Of Your Bliss is the second solo album of Norwegian band a-ha's Magne Furuholmen as Magne F.

Martin Halla | Martin Halla (born 4 June 1988 in Aurskog, Norway) is a Norwegian singer who, on 25 May 2012, won the inaugural series of "The Voice – Norges beste stemme", the Norwegian version of "The Voice" in 2012 and was broadcast on TV2.  He was part of "Team Magne" as he was mentored by Magne Furuholmen.
I Call Your Name (A-ha song) | "I Call Your Name" is a song by A-ha.  The second single from their album "East of the Sun, West of the Moon" was written by Paul Waaktaar Savoy and Magne Furuholmen.

List of A-ha tours | A-ha is a Norwegian synthpop act, founded in 1982 by Morten Harket, Magne Furuholmen and Paul Waaktaar-Savoy in Oslo.  Since 1985, A-ha has performed throughout the world, including tours in North America, South America, Europe, Australia, Japan, and China.

Apparatjik | Apparatjik ( ) are a multi-national band formed in 2008.  The band is a supergroup that consists of bassist Guy Berryman from Coldplay, guitarist/keyboardist Magne Furuholmen from A-ha, singer/guitarist Jonas Bjerre of Mew and drummer/producer Martin Terefe.  The band's name literal translation is "agent of the apparatus".  Apparatjik is based on apparatchik, which is a word of Russian origin and is used to describe "an official or bureaucrat in any organization" but has in the past had other meanings, including "Communist agent or spy".
hop2_titles: A Dot of Black in the Blue of Your Bliss
response.summary_2: Magne Furuholmen — A-ha's keyboardist and founding member — is Norwegian. Magne Furuholmen is a Norwegian musician and visual artist who co-wrote many of A-ha's hits.
